title: The cities in Europe primed for the AI revolution
author: Keith Mason
text:
RESEARCH BRIEFING

24 Jun 2026

The cities in Europe primed for the AI revolution

Discover the disparities in AI readiness across Europe, highlighting key regions
and their potential in the evolving technological landscape.

The AI Advantage Index reveals significant disparities in AI readiness across
Europe, highlighting the Nordics and North-West Europe as leaders in this
technological revolution. In contrast, Southern and Eastern Europe show a
concentration of AI capabilities in a few capital cities.

The index evaluates regions based on five key dimensions: current AI adoption,
human capital, innovation and investment, infrastructure, and labour market
structure. Notably, cities like Stockholm, Paris, and Helsinki rank at the top,
while regions such as Madrid and Lisbon illustrate a capital-centric model that
diverges from national trends.

Interestingly, advanced manufacturing hubs like Munich and Stuttgart also
demonstrate strong AI potential, suggesting that the relationship between AI
advantage and economic structure is more nuanced than previously thought. This
indicates that both service-oriented and advanced manufacturing regions can
thrive in an AI-driven landscape.
